title: pelargonium × farburghii
intro: Pelargonium × farburghii, also known as Faragher's rose, is a hybrid plant species within the geranium family, derived from the cross between Pelargonium linifolium and Pelargonium capitatum. This plant is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its antiseptic, antibacterial, and anti-inflammatory effects. The health benefits of Pelargonium × farburghii include its ability to soothe coughs, reduce fever, and alleviate digestive issues, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Pelargonium × farburghii can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, tinctures, and infusions, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties.
